excerpt: Complete breakdown of E8 Markets pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
E8 Markets Pricing Overview

E8 Markets is a proprietary trading firm that stands out for its transparent, tiered pricing structure and generous free trial. With evaluation account prices starting at just $38/month for a $5K account and scaling up to $798/month for a $200K account, E8 Markets positions itself as an accessible entry point into prop trading. What sets E8 Markets apart is the 14-day free trial with no credit card required—a rarity in the prop firm industry. For forex traders, futures enthusiasts, and experienced traders seeking funded accounts with fast payouts (24–48 hours), E8 Markets offers competitive pricing backed by flexible profit-split configurations of up to 100%.

The firm was founded in 2021 and is based in Dallas, Texas, making it a relatively young but US-established player in proprietary trading. The pricing model is simple: you pay for an evaluation account, complete trading challenges with specified drawdown limits, and if successful, you're funded with real capital (up to $1M) and can earn profit splits on your trades.

All E8 Markets Plans Compared

E8 Markets offers five distinct pricing tiers, each designed for different account sizes and capital requirements. Here's the complete breakdown:

Plan Monthly Cost Account Size Best For
Free Trial $0 Demo Account Testing the platform
$5K Account $38/month $5,000 Beginners, small account traders
$25K Account $188/month $25,000 Intermediate traders
$100K Account $448/month $100,000 Experienced traders
$200K Account $798/month $200,000 Advanced traders, volume traders

What You Get With Each Plan:

  • $5K Account ($38/mo): Perfect for traders new to E8 Markets. You get access to all supported platforms (MT5, cTrader, MatchTrader, TradeLocker), paper trading with simulated capital, custom indicators, automated trading capabilities, and full performance analytics. The evaluation challenge is straightforward with no minimum trading days required.
  • $25K Account ($188/mo): A middle-ground option for traders ready to scale. This tier includes everything in the $5K plan but with significantly larger trading capital, allowing for bigger position sizes and diversification across forex, futures, crypto, indices, and commodities.
  • $100K Account ($448/mo): Designed for serious traders. This is where you unlock meaningful profit-split potential. The larger account size allows professional-level trading strategies and more substantial daily profit opportunities.
  • $200K Account ($798/mo): The premium tier for established traders seeking maximum capital and profit potential. At this level, your monthly evaluation fee is a modest percentage of your funded account size, making the ROI highly attractive if you pass the evaluation.

All plans include access to risk management tools, performance analytics dashboards, and E8 Markets' core features. The profit split—up to 100%—is customizable at checkout, meaning you can choose your risk-reward profile when purchasing your evaluation account.

Free Plan / Free Trial

E8 Markets offers a 14-day free trial, which is exceptionally generous compared to industry standards. Here's what makes it valuable:

  • No Credit Card Required: You can sign up and test the platform without entering payment information upfront.
  • Full Platform Access: The free trial gives you access to all supported trading platforms and core features, letting you experience the actual trading environment.
  • Paper Trading: You'll trade on simulated capital, so there's zero financial risk during the evaluation period.
  • Test Multiple Strategies: With 14 days, you have ample time to test your trading strategies, familiarize yourself with the platform, and assess whether E8 Markets suits your trading style.

Limitations: The free trial is time-limited (14 days only), and profits made during the trial don't carry over to a funded account. It's strictly a testing ground. However, if you perform well during the trial, it can provide confidence before investing in a paid evaluation account.

Is It Worth Starting With? Absolutely. The free trial is an excellent way to assess platform quality, test execution speed, and evaluate the user experience without financial commitment. For new users, this is a non-negotiable first step.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While E8 Markets' pricing is transparent, there are important considerations beyond the monthly evaluation fee:

  • Platform Fees: E8 Markets itself doesn't charge spreads or commissions on trades, but the underlying platforms (MT5, cTrader, TradeLocker, MatchTrader) may have their own fee structures. These are typically minimal but worth clarifying with support.
  • Evaluation Fees Are Non-Refundable: Once you purchase an evaluation account, the monthly fee is non-refundable. This is standard industry practice but important to understand—you're paying for access to the challenge, not for a funded account guarantee.
  • Data Feed Costs: While E8 Markets provides real-time market data through the platforms, some traders might need premium data feeds for certain assets. These would be additional costs not covered by the base pricing.
  • No Mobile App Premium: E8 Markets relies on third-party platform mobile apps, so you won't have a dedicated native app. This isn't a fee but a limitation to understand.
  • Profit Split Configurability Premium: Choosing higher profit splits (80–100%) may result in slightly stricter evaluation conditions, though this isn't an explicit additional fee.

The good news: there are no surprise fees, hidden charges, or mandatory add-ons. What you pay monthly is what you pay.

E8 Markets Pricing vs Competitors

E8 Markets is competitively priced, but how does it stack up against other prop firms? Here's a comparison with similar firms in the space:

Firm $5K Eval $25K Eval Profit Split
E8 Markets $38/mo $188/mo Up to 100% (customizable)
Competitor A ~$49/mo ~$249/mo Up to 90%
Competitor B ~$99/mo ~$299/mo Up to 80%

Value Proposition: E8 Markets offers lower entry-point pricing ($38 vs. $49–$99 for competitors) while matching or exceeding profit-split offerings at 100%. Their 14-day free trial is also more generous than most competitors, who typically offer 7-day trials or no trial at all. For traders seeking maximum profit retention, E8 Markets' customizable 100% split is a significant advantage.

However, E8 Markets uses simulated capital only, which is a notable difference from firms offering live trading challenges. This affects how traders view the value—some see it as lower risk, others as less "real" trading experience.

Is E8 Markets Worth the Price?

For Beginners ($5K Account, $38/mo): Highly recommended. At $38/month, the entry cost is negligible relative to the value: platform access, paper trading, performance analytics, and a potential path to a $5K funded account. Even if you don't pass the evaluation immediately, the monthly cost is low enough to justify multiple attempts.

For Intermediate Traders ($25K Account, $188/mo): Worth it if you're serious about prop trading. At ~$2.25/day, this investment pays for itself with just a few winning trades on a $25K account. The unlimited profit split potential (up to 100%) makes this attractive for traders ready to scale.

For Experienced Traders ($100K–$200K Accounts, $448–$798/mo): Absolutely worth it. At these levels, your monthly fee is a fraction of typical daily profit potential. If you're consistently profitable, the evaluation fee is negligible against your profit-split earnings.

When You Should Upgrade: Upgrade when your consistent daily profits on your current funded account exceed the evaluation fee for the next tier. For example, if you're earning $50+/day on a $25K account, a $100K account at $448/month becomes highly attractive because your profit potential scales exponentially.

The Bottom Line: E8 Markets is fairly priced relative to the opportunity. The combination of low entry costs, transparent pricing, generous free trial, customizable profit splits, and fast payouts creates strong value for serious traders.

How to Save on E8 Markets

E8 Markets doesn't publicly advertise extensive discount programs, but here are legitimate ways to optimize your investment:

  • Use the Free Trial First: Spend 14 days testing the platform thoroughly. This eliminates wasted evaluation fees on accounts you're not committed to.
  • Master One Tier Before Scaling: Rather than jumping to the $100K account immediately, become consistently profitable on the $25K tier first. This maximizes your ROI and proves your strategy's viability.
  • Combine Multiple Accounts Strategically: Some traders run multiple smaller accounts (e.g., three $25K accounts) instead of one $100K account. This diversifies strategy testing and risk.
  • Check for Promotional Periods: Prop firms occasionally run promotions around market events or seasons. Subscribe to E8 Markets' updates to catch limited-time discounts.
  • Referral Programs: Many prop firms offer referral bonuses. If E8 Markets has a referral program, recommending friends could earn account credits.

While E8 Markets' pricing is already competitive, the most effective way to "save" is to be profitable quickly—the faster you pass evaluations and move to funded accounts, the more your profit splits exceed your evaluation costs.

Ready to evaluate E8 Markets for yourself? Read the full E8 Markets review or compare pricing across all prop firms to make the best decision for your trading goals.
